top_findings.title: MCP server doesn't handle real-time API fallback
top_findings.evidence: server.py only uses bundled CSV data; no API-Football or TheSportsDB integration
top_findings.suggestion: Optional enhancement per spec: add real-time data via API-Football (free tier with rate limits) for current season queries

top_findings.title: 15 MCP tools exposed via FastMCP
top_findings.evidence: server.py: find_matches, head_to_head, team_record, team_summary, compare_teams, find_players, get_player, club_squad, standings, season_results, list_competitions, competition_stats, biggest_wins, best_records, top_scoring_teams
top_findings.suggestion: All required capabilities are exposed as callable MCP tools

top_findings.title: All performance targets met
top_findings.evidence: README: simple lookups < 10ms, aggregate queries < 5ms; test suite completes in 1.10s
top_findings.suggestion: Performance is well under the 2s/5s SLA targets

top_findings.title: Comprehensive test coverage with 52 passing tests
top_findings.evidence: pytest output: 52 passed in 1.10s; includes BDD scenarios (test_competitions_bdd.py, test_matches_bdd.py, test_players_bdd.py, test_statistics_bdd.py, test_teams_bdd.py) and unit tests
top_findings.suggestion: Test suite covers all major capabilities and edge cases; 0 skipped tests

top_findings.title: Intelligent deduplication across overlapping datasets
top_findings.evidence: data_loader.py: 2019 Brasileirão appears in 3 files; fixtures are keyed by (competition, season, home, away) and deduplicated with ±1-day offset tolerance
top_findings.suggestion: Ensures accurate aggregates even when the same match exists in multiple source CSVs
